UseFor: Accessibility requirements, keyboard/focus behavior, forms, target size, error handling, authentication usability.
CodexInstruction: Treat WCAG 2.2 AA as the default accessibility floor. Do not build UI that breaks keyboard navigation, visible focus, contrast, labels, or error recovery.
BestForProject: All web apps, admin panels, SaaS dashboards, internal tools.
Notes: Use as acceptance criteria, not visual inspiration.

UseFor: Practical QA checklist for accessibility review.
CodexInstruction: Use this checklist when reviewing generated UI. Verify headings, landmarks, forms, dynamic content, keyboard use, widgets, media, and error states.
BestForProject: Final UI review, pull request checklist, regression testing.
Notes: Good repo-side reference for agents because it is checklist-shaped.

UseFor: General UX sanity check: status visibility, user control, consistency, error prevention, recognition over recall, minimalist design.
CodexInstruction: When making UX decisions, prefer clarity, recovery, consistency, and visible system state over decorative complexity.
BestForProject: Every product screen, especially complex workflows.
Notes: Use as the simplest rule set for judging whether a UI is usable.

UseFor: Enterprise SaaS, admin portals, data-heavy dashboards, forms, tables, filtering, navigation, layout.
CodexInstruction: For dense business UI, imitate Carbon-style spacing, tables, panels, hierarchy, component discipline, and restrained enterprise visual language.
BestForProject: Securityzator, MSP tools, reporting dashboards, admin portals.
Notes: Best primary visual and structural reference for serious enterprise dashboards.

UseFor: Microsoft/M365-style admin UX, productivity tools, app shells, navigation, tokens, controls.
CodexInstruction: When the app touches Microsoft 365, Entra, Defender, Intune, or productivity tooling, follow Fluent 2-style layout, controls, and interaction logic.
BestForProject: M365 dashboards, security-control visualizers, internal Microsoft-aligned SaaS.
Notes: Best reference when the product should feel native to Microsoft-heavy environments.

UseFor: Modern React/Tailwind components that are easy for AI agents to inspect and modify.
CodexInstruction: Prefer shadcn-style component composition: readable source, accessible primitives, Tailwind tokens, clean cards, dialogs, tables, forms, and command menus.
BestForProject: Codex-built prototypes, React dashboards, internal tools.
Notes: Best AI-friendly UI repo/reference because components are copyable source, not black-box package magic.

UseFor: Accessible low-level UI behavior: dialogs, dropdowns, tabs, popovers, keyboard behavior, focus management.
CodexInstruction: Use Radix-style behavior for interactive primitives. Do not hand-roll fragile focus traps, dropdown keyboard logic, modal behavior, or ARIA-heavy widgets.

UseFor: Developer tools, repository browsers, issue/PR UIs, technical SaaS, command surfaces.
CodexInstruction: For technical interfaces, imitate Primer-style clarity: compact controls, clear hierarchy, useful empty states, restrained icons, practical navigation.
BestForProject: Repo tools, Codex-side dashboards, dev/admin panels.

UseFor: Forms, validation, content clarity, accessibility, service patterns, plain-language UX.
CodexInstruction: For forms and workflows, prefer GOV.UK-style clarity: obvious labels, plain text, direct errors, no mystery navigation, no decorative interference.
BestForProject: Forms, onboarding, configuration flows, compliance tooling.
Notes: Not visually flashy, but excellent for making UI actually usable.

UseFor: Accessible public-sector components, responsive patterns, forms, layout, visual style guide.
CodexInstruction: Use USWDS as a secondary accessible-component reference for public-sector or compliance-heavy screens.
BestForProject: Compliance portals, structured forms, service UX.
Notes: Useful when you want accessibility discipline without enterprise SaaS gloss.

UseFor: Issue tracking, project management, tickets, operational dashboards, collaboration-heavy workflows.
CodexInstruction: For ticketing, boards, filters, statuses, actions, and collaboration screens, use Atlassian-style patterns as a reference.
BestForProject: MSP tooling, ConnectWise-style dashboards, task/ticket systems.
Notes: Good reference for operational workflow UI.

UseFor: Modern components, typography, motion, color, layout, adaptive UI.
CodexInstruction: Use Material 3 for modern polish, layout rhythm, component states, and motion restraint, but avoid making enterprise tools look like consumer mobile apps.
BestForProject: Modern web apps, responsive UI, visual polish.
Notes: Good secondary visual reference, not always ideal for dense enterprise data.

UseFor: Content-first hierarchy, layering, translucency, adaptive materials, restrained motion, platform polish.
CodexInstruction: Use Apple HIG ideas for hierarchy, subtle depth, and content-first polish. Do not blindly copy Apple visuals into dense admin dashboards.
Notes: Useful for visual taste; less useful as a web admin implementation spec.

UseFor: UX process, research, personas, design systems, accessibility, mobile/web guidance, security, post-launch optimization.
CodexInstruction: Use this when producing formal UX rationale, product process notes, or design documentation for stakeholders.
BestForProject: Documentation, formal guidelines, stakeholder-friendly UX justification.
Notes: Large reference; not something to read on every code generation pass.

SourceName: MOSIP User Interface Design Guidelines PDF
UseFor: Inclusive UI, task clarity, multiple ways to complete goals, usability, visual coherence.
CodexInstruction: Use as a compact inclusive-design checklist when simplifying complex screens for mixed-skill users.
Notes: Useful but secondary to WCAG, Deque, Carbon, and GOV.UK.

SourceName: UI Design System Guidelines PDF by Jonelle Boyd
UseFor: Practical enterprise UI patterns: badges, tables, actions columns, copy buttons, sorting, WCAG AA reminders.
CodexInstruction: Use as practical field notes for dashboard/table ergonomics, not as an authoritative standard.
BestForProject: Table-heavy admin UI and quick UI reviews.
Notes: Treat as useful notes, not scripture.

UseFor: Inspect enterprise-grade component implementation and docs.
CodexInstruction: When building enterprise components, inspect Carbon implementation patterns before inventing new component behavior.

UseFor: Inspect service-pattern docs and implementation structure.
CodexInstruction: Use for plain-language forms, validation, and structured service patterns.
